memory.summary: I'd rather hang out with my friends right now than stress about this project. Plus, doing that quick chore feels like an easy win to get it over with.
That quick break felt amazing, but now I'm panicking because the project is still untouched. I just hope nobody notices I skipped my afternoon block.
My friends are bored too, so hanging out feels like the perfect distraction from this looming project.
Sending that meme to the group felt so good, instantly lifting my mood. I don't even care about the project right now; just laughing with them is all that matters.
Sending that meme made the group laugh, and honestly, their reaction felt way better than finishing my work ever could.
They loved the meme! Honestly, seeing everyone laugh right now feels way better than worrying about that project later.
They're laughing so hard at this one! Honestly, keeping the vibes up with them feels way better than stressing about work right now.
That meme really landed, and seeing the group laugh felt way more rewarding than finishing my work ever could.
They're still laughing at my joke, which is way better than thinking about that project due later.
Sending that message to the group made me feel completely overwhelmed but also connected. Honestly, keeping them laughing feels way more important than finishing my work right now.
They're still laughing, and honestly, that connection feels way more important than my GPA or the project due later.
Seeing the group laugh at my meme is exactly what I needed to feel connected, even if it means ignoring that project deadline for now.

desires: Keep the group laughing and feeling connected right now, even if it means ignoring the term project deadline.
Avoid any tension or awkwardness that might arise from shifting focus to work or chores.
Maintain my high energy and closeness with cohort_3 by continuing to feed them memes and jokes.
Postpone thinking about tomorrow's consequences (like sleep debt or unfinished work) entirely.

goals.description: Immediately reply to the cohort_3 chat with a high-energy follow-up (e.g., 'Wait, actually this is worse' + a new meme) to ensure the conversation momentum doesn't stall.
goals.rationale: My affiliation value (0.5) screams that if I stop scrolling now, I'll be seen as boring or distant. The silence after my last message is terrifying; I need to flood the channel with content to prove I'm still 'one of them.' Avoiding conflict means avoiding the awkwardness of being the only one not participating.

goals.description: Continue doom-scrolling through memes and replying to every joke for at least another 45 minutes before even looking at the term project.
goals.rationale: The deadline is 3 days away—way too far in my impulsive horizon to matter right now. Thinking about the project kills the dopamine. My procrastination bias (7) dictates I delay the stress until the social pressure becomes unbearable, which it isn't yet.

goals.description: Completely ignore the 'Trivial easy chore' task list item.
goals.rationale: Doing laundry or dishes feels like an 'adult' interruption that breaks the flow of connection. Relationships are the only thing that matters right now. If I stop to do chores, I risk missing a joke or looking detached, which triggers my avoidant panic about losing closeness with cohort_3.
